Title: Metabolic engineering of narrow-leafed lupin for the production of enantiomerically pure (−)-sparteine

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: (−)-Sparteine is a valuable chiral auxiliary molecule for industrial asymmetric synthesis; quinolizidine alkaloids are antinutritional alkaloids in lupin seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Major QA branch from (−)-sparteine to (+)-lupanine; also contributes to minor branch involving α-isosparteine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in leaves; expression pattern resembles LDC; higher in high-QA cultivar Oskar than low-QA cultivar Tanjil by candidate-selection criteria
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in young leaves of Oskar; function assayed in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with exogenous (−)-sparteine feeding; field-grown and cabinet-grown seeds analyzed in mutant and WT lupin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 2-hydroxysparteine (proposed immediate product); didehydrosparteinium ion observed as spontaneous derivative in assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates (−)-sparteine at position 2 to form proposed 2-hydroxysparteine, initiating conversion toward (+)-lupanine and most downstream quinolizidine alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream branch-entry step from sparteine toward lupanine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts the precursor pool away from (−)-sparteine and toward (+)-lupanine and most quinolizidine alkaloids; knockout causes direct accumulation of the target product (−)-sparteine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/branch-entry step supported by knockout causing major pathway rerouting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CYP71D189 causes strong accumulation of (−)-sparteine in seeds, where it reaches 96% of total alkaloid content and ~1.2% seed dry weight, while major lupanine-derived alkaloids are strongly depleted.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate co-expression analysis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with substrate feeding and LC–MS; knockout isolation from FIND-IT/EMS mutant library; seed metabolite profiling by LC–MS; chiral GC–MS of accumulated sparteine
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq co-expression analysis using NCBI BioProject PRJNA386115 and PRJNA248164; metabolomics by LC–MS and GC–MS
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-USER transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent non-GMO metabolic-engineering target for producing enantiomerically pure (−)-sparteine in lupin seeds by knockout. Useful where transformation is difficult; can be deployed through FIND-IT/EMS selection. Likely best as a loss-of-function breeding target rather than overexpression target.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No visible phenotype or apparent yield penalty observed in small-scale experiments.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A specific EMS allele carried G-to-A at coding position 647, causing W216Stop. Data support that (−)-sparteine is precursor of most QAs in narrow-leafed lupin. CYP71A168 and CYP76E36 were tested but showed no activity in this conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D189 was identified by co-expression with LDC as the oxidase that initiates conversion of (−)-sparteine in narrow-leafed lupin. 2. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana converted fed (−)-sparteine into a putative didehydrosparteinium ion, consistent with formation of 2-hydroxysparteine. 3. Knockout of CYP71D189 in lupin seeds almost abolished major lupanine-derived alkaloids and caused high accumulation of enantiomerically pure (−)-sparteine, demonstrating this enzyme is decisive for flux from sparteine to downstream QAs.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Quinolizidine alkaloids are antinutritional in lupin seeds; lupanine is a major QA in bitter cultivars.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Major QA branch from (−)-sparteine via 2-hydroxysparteine to (+)-lupanine; also implicated in minor α-isosparteine route
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in leaves; higher in leaves of high-QA cultivar Oskar than low-QA cultivar Tanjil; selected as the only SDR candidate meeting leaf/high-QA/specificity criteria
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with CYP71D189 in N. benthamiana with exogenous (−)-sparteine feeding
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts on the immediate CYP71D189 product, proposed 2-hydroxysparteine, to generate (+)-lupanine during stepwise oxidation of (−)-sparteine.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream oxidation step immediately downstream of CYP71D189
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a major downstream alkaloid product from the immediate precursor generated by CYP71D189
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with CYP71D189, enables production of (+)-lupanine from fed (−)-sparteine in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome-guided candidate selection; transient co-expression with CYP71D189 in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; LC–MS detection of decreased didehydrosparteinium and appearance of (+)-lupanine
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq candidate selection using NCBI BioProject PRJNA386115 and PRJNA248164; metabolite profiling by LC–MS
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-USER transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target for reconstructing or tuning lupanine-forming QA pathways; less suitable than CYP71D189 knockout for sparteine accumulation because its activity promotes conversion away from sparteine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The transcript was specifically annotated as (−)-isopiperitenol/(−)-carveol dehydrogenase by sequence similarity, but in this study its QA-pathway role is proposed from functional co-expression evidence. No standalone knockout phenotype in lupin was reported.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SDR1 was the only SDR candidate satisfying expression and specificity filters for QA biosynthesis. 2. Co-expression of SDR1 with CYP71D189 in Nicotiana benthamiana enabled formation of (+)-lupanine from fed (−)-sparteine. 3. SDR1 likely acts on 2-hydroxysparteine, the immediate product of CYP71D189, in the major route from sparteine to lupanine.
